title: Unit and integration tests

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Unit and integration tests; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Software Development Fundamentals; August 2023; provisions applicable to Unit and integration tests
sources.claim: C++ tests assert observable contracts across normal, boundary, failure, lifetime, and exception cases without coupling unnecessarily to private representation.

sources.locator: C++ Core Guidelines; living guidelines accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Unit and integration tests
sources.claim: C++ tests assert observable contracts across normal, boundary, failure, lifetime, and exception cases without coupling unnecessarily to private representation.

canonicalExplanation.summary: C++ tests assert observable contracts across normal, boundary, failure, lifetime, and exception cases without coupling unnecessarily to private representation.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A parser test covers empty, valid, boundary, and malformed input and checks returned value or error rather than private helper call order. Your goal is to create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Conceptual constraints and diagnostics; Exceptions and guarantees; Streams, files and parsing; Class interface and invariants. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: C++ tests assert observable contracts across normal, boundary, failure, lifetime, and exception cases without coupling unnecessarily to private representation. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Undefined behavior can invalidate a test, nondeterminism needs control, death and exception tests are tool-specific, and one implementation-shaped oracle can reject valid alternatives. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.

includes: Design deterministic tests for values, classes, exceptions and file boundaries.
Create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases.
excludes: Undefined behavior can invalidate a test, nondeterminism needs control, death and exception tests are tool-specific, and one implementation-shaped oracle can reject valid alternatives.

outcomes: Create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases.

examples.title: Unit and integration tests: defined case
examples.situation: A parser test covers empty, valid, boundary, and malformed input and checks returned value or error rather than private helper call order.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A parser test covers empty, valid, boundary, and malformed input and checks returned value or error rather than private helper call order.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.

examples.title: Unit and integration tests: boundary case
examples.situation: A move-only resource test verifies ownership and cleanup behavior without assuming a moved-from container is empty unless documented.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A move-only resource test verifies ownership and cleanup behavior without assuming a moved-from container is empty unless documented.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Undefined behavior can invalidate a test, nondeterminism needs control, death and exception tests are tool-specific, and one implementation-shaped oracle can reject valid alternatives.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.
misconceptions.correction: Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Unit and integration tests: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Undefined behavior can invalidate a test, nondeterminism needs control, death and exception tests are tool-specific, and one implementation-shaped oracle can reject valid alternatives.

recap.summary: You should now be able to create a focused automated test suite with normal, boundary and failure cases. Keep this boundary in view: Undefined behavior can invalidate a test, nondeterminism needs control, death and exception tests are tool-specific, and one implementation-shaped oracle can reject valid alternatives.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Unit and integration tests using a concrete CPP rule rather than the optional analogy.
Why is this claim incorrect: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.
Apply the checkpoint 'contracts' to the boundary case from memory.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “CMake project structure”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
